abstract: In this paper, we present the Deep Gradient Network (DGNet), a novel deep learning framework designed to leverage object gradient supervision for camouflaged object detection (COD). The architecture uniquely decouples the detection process into two interconnected branches: a context encoder and a texture encoder. At the heart of this framework is a gradient-induced transition mechanism, which facilitates a "soft grouping" between contextual and textural features to better isolate hidden targets. Despite its architectural simplicity, DGNet significantly outperforms current state-of-the-art COD models across multiple benchmarks. Specifically, our streamlined version, DGNet-S, achieves real-time inference speeds of 80 fps while delivering performance comparable to the industry-leading JCSOD-CVPR21 model, despite utilizing only 6.82% of its parameters. Furthermore, we demonstrate the versatility of DGNet by achieving robust results in downstream applications, including polyp segmentation, industrial defect detection, and transparent object segmentation. Our code and pre-trained models are available at https://github.com/GewelsJI/DGNet.
